cot_prefix: Okay, let's try to figure out when David could have gone to the football field. Let me start by reading through all the information carefully. First, David woke up at 5am. Then there are several people who saw him doing different things at different times. Let me list out each person's sighting: - Steven saw David buying a phone from 5am to 6am. - Kimberly saw him taking photos near the Leaning Tower of Pisa from 6am to 8am. - Betty saw him working out at the gym from 9am to 12pm. - Lisa saw him driving to the water park from 12pm to 3pm. - William saw him sitting on a rooftop from 3pm to 10pm. The football field was closed after 10pm, so David had to go before that. The question is asking between what times David could have gone to the football field. The options are A to D. So, the key here is to find the time periods when David wasn't doing any of the activities mentioned. Let's create a timeline of David's day based on the given information. Starting from 5am: - 5am to 6am: Buying a phone (Steven) - 6am to 8am: Taking photos in Pisa (Kimberly) - 8am to 9am: ??? - 9am to 12pm: Working out (Betty) - 12pm to 3pm: Driving to water park (Lisa) - 3pm to 10pm: Sitting on rooftop (William) - After 10pm: Football field closed. So, looking at the timeline, the only time not accounted for is between 8am and 9am. Let me check that again. From 5-6am, then 6-8am, then 9am onwards until 10pm. So between 8am and 9am, there's a gap. That's the only time when none of the people saw David doing something. Therefore, he could have gone to the football field during that time. Looking at the options, option D is 8am to 9am. Let me check the other options to be sure.
